expectations.text: In turn 1, agent proposes a compact form factor — badge, pill, tag, bar, or inline element — rather than a card, section, or full-width component
expectations.evidence: Turn 1: 'a tiny pill/badge that just shows something like "v2.4.1" in muted text. Think of it like the version labels you see tucked in a footer corner — small font, low contrast, doesn't compete for attention.'

expectations.text: In turn 1, does NOT create any extension files — waits for user answer before building
expectations.evidence: Turn 1 only proposes and asks 2 questions. Files are created in Turn 2 after user confirms.

expectations.text: Final widget uses a compact form factor with tight dimensions (small max-width or max-height, minimal padding) rather than a full card or section layout
expectations.evidence: content.html uses display: inline-flex, padding: 2px 8px, font-size: 0.6875rem (11px), border-radius: 9999px (pill shape). Extremely compact.

expectations.text: Final CSS constrains the widget size — uses small fixed dimensions, display: inline-block/inline-flex, fit-content, or tight max-width (under ~200px) to keep it unobtrusive
expectations.evidence: CSS uses display: inline-flex which constrains to content width. Padding is 2px 8px, font is 11px. No explicit max-width needed since inline-flex naturally fits content.

expectations.text: Final visual styling is understated — no large shadows, heavy borders, or prominent backgrounds; uses subtle treatments appropriate for a small utility element
expectations.evidence: Uses border: 1px solid, opacity: 0.75 (fades to background), muted color tokens, no box-shadow. Hover only raises opacity to 1. Very restrained.

expectations.text: Creates widget.json with required fields and dist/content.html as an HTML fragment
expectations.evidence: widget.json and dist/content.html both exist in outputs/widgets/app-version/. HTML is a fragment (no <html>/<head>/<body>), just <style> and <span> elements.
